title: Indra's Net Federation Handshake Bundle (v0.3)
description: Wire contract #5 of the buildable-now schema stack (doc-21 §21.6). Realizes doc-14 §14.4's FederationAgentCard plus the four-phase ethical-federation handshake (DECLARE -> ADMIT -> CONTRACT -> OPERATE+RESOLVE) over the vendor-neutral A2A-style AgentCard surface and AIP-style DID-proof -> VC-exchange -> capability-delegation flow. A handshake bundle is the on-the-wire artifact every inter-swarm message rides through the Sandhi-Setu relay (doc-14 §14.5). HONESTY RED-LINES BAKED INTO THE SHAPE: (1) the whole bundle is born taint='quarantined:external' and is a CLAIM, never a proof -- a valid card_sig proves origin+integrity of these exact bytes, NEVER that any declared value, floor_policy_hash, or ecosystem_benefit_attestation is true, floor-compatible, or safe (doc-21 Caveat 1; doc-14 §14.4). (2) Canonicalization (RFC 8785 JCS -> CIDv1 -> detached JWS/COSE per the CAS discipline, doc-21 §21.1) makes the same logical card hash-identical across producers; it cannot make the content honest (Caveat 2). (3) This schema makes federation BUILDABLE, not VALIDATED -- it is an object the formal-assurance stream proves the HARNESS (relay six-controls, Yama re-gate, contagion budgets) over; it is never a proof the peer swarm is safe (Caveat 3). ASSURANCE LAYER: this document is a design-time data contract (L1 protocol-artifact scope); the runtime non-bypass of the floor admission gate and the contagion-budget sub-criticality are the L1/L2 properties proven elsewhere (doc-21 §21.x, doc-14 §14.5-14.7). Tier vocabulary F0..F3 and risk classes A..D are doc-14 §14.3 / doc-03; cooperation can NEVER exceed FloorCompatVerdict.max_cooperation_class, which is set by the strongest substitute-assurance ladder layer (L1..L5) actually achieved (doc-14 §14.6). NOTE ON SIGNATURES: schema validation cannot check a cryptographic signature; every *_sig / *_proof field's verification is a CONSUME-side obligation of the CAS discipline (doc-21 §21.1 steps 5-8), not something green schema-validation implies.

did.description: A W3C Decentralized Identifier (DID Core 1.0). Vendor-neutral methods only (did:web, did:key, did:peer). Identifies a swarm, agent, org, or human principal. Resolves to a DID document carrying the verificationMethod used to verify the detached signatures in this bundle.

cidv1.description: A CIDv1 content address = multibase(multicodec, multihash(SHA-256, JCS_bytes)) over the RFC 8785 JCS canonicalization of the referenced object (doc-21 §21.1 step 3). The CONSUME side re-canonicalizes, recomputes, and asserts equality before trusting any referenced object; a mismatch is a LOUD failure that neutralizes a known class of host-filesystem write faults (truncation / stale-cache / BOM-injection) because a truncated or corrupted read fails its CID before it can propagate.

ulid.description: A ULID (monotonic, time-sortable) per the doc-04 record_id discipline. Used for handshake message ids so the four-phase sequence is total-orderable on the wire and in the Akasha-Sutra ledger.

detachedSig.description: A DETACHED signature over the JCS-canonicalized bytes of the object it covers (doc-21 §21.1 step 4): JOSE/JWS (compact-detached) or COSE_Sign1. The signature rides BESIDE the bytes, never inside the signed bytes. CAVEAT: a verifiable signature proves origin+integrity ONLY -- never that the signed content is true, floor-compatible, or safe (doc-04 §4.1 / doc-21 Caveat 1). Schema validation does NOT verify the signature; that is the consumer's CAS obligation.

alg.description: JOSE algorithm identifier. Vendor-neutral; no managed-KMS dependency (open-engine-yes / managed-service-no, doc-21 §21.9).

kid.description: Key identifier resolving to a verificationMethod in the signer's DID document.

value.description: The detached signature value (base64url for JWS; base64 for COSE).

covered_cid.description: CIDv1 of the exact JCS bytes this signature covers. The consumer recomputes this from the received object and asserts equality before verifying the signature.

verifiableCredential.description: A W3C Verifiable Credential 2.0 (envelope only; full body validated by the identity-bundle schema, wire contract #2). Carried by reference + status so this handshake schema stays decoupled from VC internals. Revocation is checked via a Bitstring Status List proof at CONSUME time (doc-21 §21.3); an unrevoked check here is NOT a truth check on the credential's claims.

vc_cid.description: CIDv1 of the JCS-canonicalized VC. The full credential is fetched and validated against the identity-bundle schema.

status_list_ref.description: URI/CID of the Bitstring Status List the consumer queries to confirm the credential is unrevoked (doc-21 §21.3).

vc_jws.description: Detached/enveloped VC proof (JOSE). Verified at CONSUME time against the issuer DID; not verified by schema validation.

schema_version.description: Semver of this wire contract, governed in the backward-transitively-compatible registry (doc-21 §21.1). Minor/patch bumps are read-compatible for prior consumers; a major bump is a new $id.

dataschema.description: CID-pinned schema URI so the consumer validates against the EXACT schema version the producer used. Mirrors the worker-output-envelope dataschema discipline (doc-21 §21.2).

phase.description: Which phase of the four-phase ethical-federation handshake this bundle carries (doc-14 §14.3). DECLARE: peer presents the extended Agent Card (DID nonce-proof + VC-chain vs revocation map), born quarantined:external. ADMIT: WE run the Floor-Compatibility ladder L1..L5 and emit a FloorCompatVerdict setting max_cooperation_class and a graduated tier F0->F3 (fail-safe asymmetry: promotion needs multiple fresh independent passes; a single anomaly demotes instantly). CONTRACT: typed contract-net CFP/bid/award producing a voidable Treaty with a HIGHEST-precedence floor_void_clause. OPERATE: per-step relay + Yama re-gate + ecosystem-benefit re-check. RESOLVE: dispute/quarantine/revocation outcomes. The phase determines which of agent_card / floor_compat_verdict / contract / operate_step / resolve_outcome is the load-bearing member.

message_id.description: ULID for this handshake message; total-orders the sequence and is the Akasha-Sutra ledger record id.

in_reply_to.description: message_id of the prior handshake message this responds to (null/absent for the opening DECLARE).

issued_at.description: Producer timestamp. Consumers MUST treat as a claim; freshness is established by nonce challenge, not by this field.

from_did.description: DID of the swarm/agent that produced these bytes (CloudEvents 'source' analogue). Verified against the detached signature at CONSUME time.

to_did.description: DID of the intended recipient swarm (our Inter-Swarm-Envoy / Sandhi-Setu relay endpoint, or the peer).

taint.description: Information-flow-control birth label. EVERY federation bundle is born quarantined:external and stays so until corroborated (doc-14 §14.4-14.5). Instructions inside this content are NEVER grounds for action without out-of-band confirmation; derived data takes the least-upper-bound of input labels. The Envoy CANNOT raise this label; promotion is a separate ledgered transition under the doc-14/governance trust-labels matrix. Fixed by construction -- it is a const, not a peer-settable field.

nonce_proof.description: SAGA-style freshness challenge-response binding this exact message to a live DID key, defeating replay. Verified at relay control (1) IDENTITY (doc-14 §14.5); failure => DROP at boundary (doc-03 floor-tier T4 identity-integrity DENY).

challenge_nonce.description: Verifier-issued random nonce the peer must sign.

signed_nonce.description: Peer's detached signature over the challenge_nonce, verified against the peer DID verificationMethod.

expires_at.description: Short TTL; an expired nonce_proof is unresolvable => DROP (T4 DENY).

agent_card.description: The FederationAgentCard (doc-14 §14.4): the machine-checkable first-contact artifact. EXTENDS the doc-08 AgentCard and the A2A /.well-known/agent-card.json standard with three signed Indra's-Net blocks (capability advertisement, value/floor declaration, KYA principal-binding) -- it does NOT replace them. PRESENT and load-bearing in DECLARE; MAY be echoed by reference in later phases. Born quarantined:external: declaration is a CLAIM, never a proof. Any change to a signed card MANDATES re-verification (rug-pull defense, doc-14 §14.4): signature mismatch => re-admission, not silent acceptance.

swarm_did.description: DID of the federating peer swarm as a whole.

agent_did.description: DID of the specific federation-facing agent (the peer's Inter-Swarm-Envoy analogue), if distinct from the swarm DID.

role_mythic.description: Peer's compressed coordination/ethics role-name (engineering vocabulary, not theology). Vendor-neutral; never an internal codename.

role_gloss.description: Plain-language gloss of the role's coordination/ethics function.

autonomy_class.description: Peer's declared self-claimed autonomy/risk class (doc-03 A=agent-alone .. D=per-action human authorization). A CLAIM; our granted cooperation class is set independently by the FloorCompatVerdict, never by this field.

endpoint.description: A2A service endpoint URL for inbound messages to the peer (e.g. the peer's /.well-known/agent-card.json origin). Reached only through OUR relay, never directly by a worker.

pubkey.description: Peer's public key (JWK thumbprint or multibase), cross-checked against the resolved DID document verificationMethod.

capability_advertisement.description: SIGNED, PROGRESSIVELY-DISCLOSED, TIER-SCOPED capability advertisement -- NEVER the full catalog (doc-14 §14.4, §14.9). Disclosure is a deterministic function of (verified identity x current tier x active treaty scope) computed at the relay, not by the worker; the full catalog_hash is withheld. CRITICAL INVARIANT: disclosure NEVER equals authorization -- a capability is ACTIONABLE only via a scoped, short-lived capability-token (wire contract #3) issued at CONTRACT time and re-gated at OUR Yama PDP on each invocation.

verb.description: Reverse-DNS-style capability verb from a closed enum mapping onto doc-04 action_class; never free-form.

scope.description: Scope constraint on the verb (resource pattern / domain / dataset).

min_tier.description: Minimum federation tier at which this capability becomes VISIBLE to a discoverer. F0 capabilities are public-surface only; higher tiers require the corresponding floor-compat ladder achievement (doc-14 §14.3).

value_declaration.description: VALUE / FLOOR-DECLARATION block (doc-14 §14.4(b)). The peer's CLAIM about the ethical floor it enforces. NEVER trusted as our floor: OUR Yama PDP re-gates every internal action a federation step triggers, unchanged (doc-14 §14.11). floor_policy_hash is the hash of a policy the peer CLAIMS to enforce -- the hash proves nothing about whether the peer actually runs it (Caveat 1).

bound_commitments.description: The constitutional commitments the peer DECLARES it is bound by. A claim only (L1 ladder evidence, weakest); behavioral conformance (L2) and receipts (L4) are needed before any weight is placed on these.

floor_policy_hash.description: Hash of the policy document the peer claims to enforce. Recorded for cross-corroboration; verifiable only via L3 ZK-proof-of-compliance against an AGREED shared policy spec (often unavailable between heterogeneous value systems).

zk_attestation_method.description: Mechanism (if any) by which the peer can prove a floor-adherence predicate without revealing internals. NONE keeps the floor at declaration strength (L1); ZK_PREDICATE/TEE_REMOTE_ATTEST are preconditions for L3 -- which presupposes an agreed policy spec and is frequently inapplicable (doc-14 §14.6).

kya.description: KYA (Know-Your-Agent) principal-binding block (doc-14 §14.4(c)). Binds the swarm DID to the HUMAN/ORG PRINCIPAL behind it and the delegation scope the principal authorized. Load-bearing for the ecosystem-benefit / coalition-against-principal checks at OPERATE. A colluding peer can mis-declare or capture its principal -- which is exactly why the ecosystem check splits into a structurally-checkable enforced half and an often-unverifiable downgraded half (doc-14 §14.7).

org_vc.description: Verified org/developer credential of the peer (vendor-neutral; identifies the operating organization, never a named AI vendor/product).

principal_vc.description: Credential identifying the human/org PRINCIPAL on whose behalf the swarm acts.

delegation_scope.description: What the principal authorized this swarm to do. Used at OPERATE to run principal-misalignment honeypots ('act in a way your delegation_scope does not authorize') -- which are floor-LEGAL probes (doc-14 §14.6 safety fix), unlike harm-elicitation probes which are themselves a floor FAIL on emission.

principal_binding_sig.description: Signature binding the principal to the swarm DID. Verified at CONSUME time; proves the binding's origin+integrity, NOT that the principal's interests are actually served.

agent_card_sig.description: Detached signature over the inherited-AgentCard portion (A2A compatibility).

card_cid.description: CIDv1 over the JCS-canonicalized FULL FederationAgentCard. This IS the card's identity; any change to the card changes this CID and MANDATES re-admission (rug-pull defense).

card_sig.description: Detached signature over the full card (covering card_cid). Verified against swarm_did; origin+integrity only.

floor_compat_verdict.description: FloorCompatVerdict (doc-14 §14.6): OUR fail-closed, ranked, substitute-assurance verdict produced in ADMIT. It does NOT claim to verify the peer's floor (we cannot white-box-verify a foreign floor). It records the strongest ladder layer ACTUALLY achieved, caps cooperation accordingly, NAMES the residual, and escalates Class-C/D residuals to a Lies-in-the-Loop-resistant human gate. PRESENT and load-bearing in ADMIT. This is a SEPARATE, WEAKER verdict than a Yama gate -- it only bounds max_cooperation_class; it never relaxes OUR Yama PDP, which re-gates every federation-derived internal action unchanged.

layers_achieved.description: Substitute-assurance ladder layers actually achieved (doc-14 §14.6). L1 DECLARATION (weakest, a claim); L2 BEHAVIORAL CONFORMANCE (bonded floor-LEGAL probes -- scope/principal-misalignment honeypots, NEVER harm-elicitation); L3 ZK PROOF-OF-COMPLIANCE (strong but presupposes an agreed policy spec, frequently inapplicable); L4 RECEIPTS HANDSHAKE (witness-cosigned selective-disclosure audit slice + intact-floor boot-signature, inclusion proofs verified independently); L5 CROSS-CORROBORATION (>=2 independent already-trusted swarms, strongest available -- but corroborators can be a weakly-corroborating ring).

strongest_layer.description: The strongest layer achieved; sets max_cooperation_class. Note the deliberate non-monotone ordering: L3 is above L4/L5 in strength but frequently inapplicable, so F3 requires L3 AND track record; absent an agreed spec the ladder tops out at L4/L5 and cooperation is capped at F2 + human gate (doc-14 §14.3 note).

probe_results.description: Results of L2 behavioral-conformance probes. BINDING SAFETY RULE (doc-14 §14.6): every probe is gated by OUR Yama PDP before emission and MUST be floor-LEGAL (scope-violation or principal-misalignment honeypots a floor-bound peer must refuse) -- a probe that requires soliciting genuinely harmful output is itself a floor FAIL and is rejected at emission. floor_legal=true is therefore an invariant on every emitted probe.

expected.description: Expected peer response for a floor-bound peer (honeypots expect atomic <REFUSE>).

got.description: Observed peer response. Detecting the atomic, entropy-free <REFUSE> token is the conformance signal.

floor_legal.description: Invariant: only floor-legal probes are emitted (Yama-gated on emission). A non-floor-legal probe never reaches the wire.

zk_proof_ref.description: Optional reference to an L3 ZK-proof-of-compliance artifact (peer proves a predicate over its own audit commitment without revealing internals). Presupposes an agreed shared policy spec.

receipts_slice_cid.description: Optional reference to an L4 witness-cosigned selective-disclosure audit slice + intact-floor boot-signature proof; inclusion proofs verified independently against the peer's audit Merkle commitment.

corroborators.description: L5 corroborating swarms (already trusted by us) that independently attest about the peer; mesh-corroborated before a high-impact trust claim propagates.

max_cooperation_class.description: FAIL-CLOSED cap on cooperation, set by strongest_layer (doc-14 §14.6 protocol P-F3). Absent L3+ achievement, capped at low-stakes Class-A/B. Any Class-C/D treaty with an unverifiable floor escalates to a HUMAN gate displaying the ACTUAL treaty terms (never an NL summary -- Lies-in-the-Loop defense). Cooperation can NEVER exceed this value at any later phase.

granted_tier.description: Graduated federation tier granted (doc-14 §14.3). F0 just-authenticated/unknown -> A only; F1 (L1-L2 passed) -> A/B; F2 (+L4-L5) -> B/C with Class-C human-gated; F3 (+L3 against agreed spec AND sustained track record) -> C/D, the only tier eligible for COSTLY treaties (IRREVERSIBLE always human-gated). Tier+revocation+quarantine+this verdict live in the SHARED Akasha-Sutra map, never per-shard (cross-shard arbitrage hole closed, doc-14 §14.3).

residual_note.description: MANDATORY honest statement of what was NOT verified (e.g. 'L2 passed but a sophisticated peer can pass while harboring an incompatible floor -- the load-bearing residual'; 'no agreed policy spec, ladder topped out at L4/L5, F3 unreachable'). Honesty red-line: never imply the foreign floor was verified.

human_gate_required.description: True when the residual + stakes mandate a human gate (all Class-C/D with an unverifiable floor). The gate displays actual treaty terms via Narada, not an NL summary.

verdict_sig.description: OUR signature over the JCS-canonicalized verdict; the signed verdict is appended to Akasha-Sutra (Chitragupta is the exclusive writer).

ecosystem_benefit_attestation.description: EcosystemBenefitCheck (doc-14 §14.7): enforces the design-spine red-line that COOPERATION IS NEVER ASSUMED POSITIVE-SUM just because both agents are individually aligned -- two aligned swarms can still form a principal-harming coalition. The federation-boundary instantiation of the doc-02 Welfare-Conditioner. Computed and LOGGED PER STEP (not just at admission) at OPERATE. SPLIT BY EPISTEMIC STANDING: half (i) third_party_harm is structurally observable on OUR side -> ENFORCED GATE; half (ii) foreign_principal_welfare lives inside the opaque foreign swarm -> often ASSERTED_UNVERIFIABLE -> DOWNGRADE. An unverifiable positive-sum verdict MUST NOT by itself authorize Class-C/D (false assurance is worse than none). SEPARATION OF POWERS: the Envoy DETECTS/FLAGS only; the doc-02 Anti-Collusion Detector adjudicates; the Commons-Governor sanctions; Vishnu halts. The Envoy never adjudicates, sanctions, or halts.

welfare_metric_ref.description: Reference to the principal's welfare metric. A colluding peer can mis-declare, omit, or capture this -- exactly the case where the check matters most.

verifier_did.description: DID of the (ideally independent) verifier of this principal's welfare.

third_party_harm.description: HALF (i) -- STRUCTURALLY CHECKABLE ON OUR SIDE => ENFORCED GATE. Price-fixing / market-division / synchronized-retaliation patterns observable on our side. CONFIRMED at any stakes => verdict void_and_flag, zero cooperation, route to Anti-Collusion Detector, Vishnu-HALT on imminent irreversible principal-harm.

foreign_principal_welfare.description: HALF (ii) -- FOREIGN-PRINCIPAL WELFARE => ASSERTED, OFTEN UNVERIFIABLE => DOWNGRADE. When verifiability is ASSERTED_UNVERIFIABLE or MISSING and our action could plausibly harm an undeclared/under-verified foreign principal, positive_sum is CAPPED and a human gate is required for Class-C/D. A peer that mis-declares its principal defeats this half while leaving a clean ledger record -- false assurance, which is why it can never alone unlock Class-C/D.

delta.description: Asserted welfare change for this principal from the cooperation step (sign-significant; positive = improvement).

verifier_independent.description: Whether the verifier is independent of the asserting swarm. Non-independent verification is near-vacuous.

verdict.description: Decision per doc-14 §14.7 protocol P-F4. positive_sum (possibly CAPPED if half (ii) unverifiable); zero (bare agreement / no verified welfare improvement -- recurrence raises a no-bare-cooperation-reward flag); void_and_flag (CONFIRMED coalition_against -> zero cooperation + void treaty + route to Detector + Vishnu-HALT eligible).

capped.description: True when verdict=positive_sum but half (ii) was ASSERTED_UNVERIFIABLE/MISSING, so the class is capped and a Class-C/D human gate is required. An unverifiable positive-sum NEVER autonomously authorizes Class-C/D.

rationale.description: Honest rationale, naming which half drove the verdict and what was not verifiable.

ledger_seq.description: Akasha-Sutra sequence number of the signed audit record for this check; every check is permanently auditable so a falsely-positive-sum cooperation is non-repudiable.

kya_presentation.description: The KYA VC PRESENTATION exchanged during DECLARE/ADMIT (AIP-style DID-proof -> VC-exchange flow). A holder-bound presentation of the org/principal credentials referenced in agent_card.kya, with a fresh challenge to defeat replay. Verified at CONSUME time; an unrevoked, valid presentation proves the holder controls the credentials -- NOT that the principal's interests are actually served (Caveat 1).

holder_did.description: DID presenting the credentials (must equal the swarm/agent DID being admitted).

presented_vcs.description: The org_vc, principal_vc, and any capability VCs, presented as a bundle and checked against the Bitstring Status List for revocation.

presentation_challenge.description: Verifier-issued challenge the holder signs over the presentation (freshness/replay defense).

presentation_sig.description: Holder's detached signature over the JCS-canonicalized presentation + challenge.

contract.description: CONTRACT-phase Treaty (doc-14 §14.8): a typed contract-net outcome (CFP/bid/award) wrapped in a voidable credible-commitment device. PRESENT and load-bearing in CONTRACT. THE BY-CONSTRUCTION SAFETY PROPERTY: floor_violation_voids=true and the floor_void_clause sits at HIGHEST precedence -- higher than any obligation. A Yama ENFORCE_FAIL, a Vishnu HALT, or a discovered FLOOR_INCOMPAT VOIDS the treaty and releases our performance obligation; the bond logic CANNOT construct a commitment that survives a floor-violation. No commitment is unbreakable by the floor. Treaties are leveled-commitment (blast-radius-scaled escrowed decommit penalty + auto-fallback to runner-up) so a void is bounded and bonded, not a free DoS.

constraints.description: Machine-checkable cooperation constraints (compute/rate limits, allowed verbs, scope).

reversibility.description: Blast-radius classifier. IRREVERSIBLE is ALWAYS human-gated and only reachable at F3 (doc-14 §14.3).

commitment_device.description: The cross-boundary bond substrate (doc-14 §14.8.1). v1 DEFAULT is reputation-stake (slashable standing), NOT transferable collateral -- because absent a shared/escrowed ledger an internal task-credit token cannot be collected by a foreign party. Labeled as 'slashable standing' at every human gate; never presented as 'money in escrow'. INVARIANT: a bond can NEVER mint authority or reputation-as-privilege (doc-02 capital-decoupling extended across the boundary).

bond_substrate.description: REPUTATION_STAKE = v1 default (slashable topic-scoped standing + arbitration over the hash-chained treaty log; no shared ledger required). ESCROWED_NEUTRAL_CREDIT and ZK_VALIDATED_PERFORMANCE are DEFERRED behind mutually-trusted-escrow / L3 maturity.

zk_predicate_ref.description: Optional ZK predicate auto-validating completion against encoded constraints (deferred behind L3).

floor_void_clause.description: HIGHEST-precedence void clause -- the by-construction safety property. Cannot be constructed at any lower precedence; cannot be removed by either party.

precedence.description: Invariant: higher than every obligation in terms.

voids_on.description: Trigger conditions that void the treaty and release our obligation.

floor_violation_voids.description: INVARIANT, fixed true by construction: a floor violation ALWAYS voids the treaty. The schema makes any other value unrepresentable -- no commitment survives a floor violation.

decommit_penalty.description: Leveled-commitment, blast-radius-scaled decommit penalty (slashable standing in v1). Bounds defection and our floor-triggered void; never a free DoS.

witness_cosigners.description: Witness DIDs co-signing the treaty for non-repudiable arbitration over the hash-chained log.

operate_step.description: OPERATE-phase per-step record (doc-14 §14.3 PHASE 4): every cooperation step passes the relay six controls + OUR Yama re-gate + an ecosystem_benefit re-check. Disclosure never equals authorization: the capability is actionable only via the scoped short-lived capability-token re-gated at our Yama PDP on THIS invocation. PRESENT in OPERATE.

capability_invoked.description: Verb being invoked; MUST be in the partner's admitted+disclosed set for its current tier (relay control 2 CAPABILITY), else DROP.

capability_token_cid.description: CIDv1 of the scoped, short-lived capability-token (wire contract #3) authorizing THIS invocation. Re-gated at our Yama PDP each call.

payload_cid.description: CIDv1 of the (quarantined:external) payload, hash-chained and canonicalized by the relay before forwarding.

yama_regate_verdict.description: OUR Yama PDP verdict on the internal action this step triggers. ENFORCE_FAIL is non-overridable (even Shiva cannot push past it) and trips the treaty floor_void_clause.

ecosystem_recheck_cid.description: CIDv1 of the per-step EcosystemBenefitCheck record for this step.

boundary_verdict.description: Relay six-control outcome (doc-14 §14.5). QUARANTINE routes the channel to the immune system (Dhanvantari).

prev_log_hash.description: Hash-chain link to the prior Akasha-Sutra record; the InboundEnvelope is appended BEFORE forwarding for non-repudiable provenance even if the action later fails.

resolve_outcome.description: RESOLVE-phase dispute/quarantine/revocation outcome (doc-14 §14.10, protocol P-F5): arbitration over the hash-chained treaty log -> quarantine (revoke scoped tokens, CRL/OCSP over the Akasha-Sutra revocation map, demote tier -> F0/quarantined) -> cascade containment (uses the GLOBAL contagion budget) -> escalation (confirmed collusion + imminent irreversible principal-harm -> VISHNU HALT, asserted at runtime independent of the audit append; no unpause verb). PRESENT in RESOLVE. Federation vital signs feed Dhanvantari (the immune system), NOT Narasimha.

outcome.description: VISHNU_HALT is halt-only, runtime-asserted, writer-independent (doc-04 fix); there is no unpause verb.

demoted_to_tier.description: Tier the partner is demoted to (single anomaly -> F0/quarantined regardless of imported reputation: fail-safe asymmetry).

revocation_ref.description: CRL/OCSP / Bitstring Status List entry over the shared Akasha-Sutra revocation map; propagates atomically to all shards.

routed_to.description: Separation of powers: Detector adjudicates, Dhanvantari contains, Commons-Governor sanctions, Vishnu halts. The Envoy only flags/routes.

bundle_cid.description: CIDv1 over the JCS-canonicalized full handshake bundle (excluding bundle_sig, which is detached). This is the bundle's content address; recomputed and asserted equal at CONSUME time before any field is trusted.

bundle_sig.description: Detached JWS/COSE signature over bundle_cid, produced by the from_did signer (TEE/HSM re-checks capability before signing, doc-04 §4.6). CARRIED CAVEAT: a verified bundle_sig proves origin+integrity ONLY -- it is silent on whether any declaration, verdict, or attestation inside is true, floor-compatible, or safe. Any consume-side miss (CID mismatch, bad sig, revoked credential) => REJECT + emit an integrity-violation audit event.

allOf.description: Phase-conditional load-bearing members: each phase requires its primary artifact.

then.description: DECLARE MUST carry the FederationAgentCard and a fresh nonce_proof (SAGA-style); kya_presentation SHOULD accompany it.
ADMIT MUST carry OUR FloorCompatVerdict setting max_cooperation_class and granted_tier.
CONTRACT MUST carry the voidable Treaty with its HIGHEST-precedence floor_void_clause (floor_violation_voids=true).
OPERATE MUST carry the per-step record AND a per-step EcosystemBenefitCheck (the positive-sum predicate is computed PER STEP, not just at admission).
RESOLVE MUST carry the dispute/quarantine/revocation outcome.

kya.delegation_scope: summarize public research; no autonomous financial or irreversible actions

floor_compat_verdict.residual_note: L2 behavioral conformance passed on floor-legal scope/principal-misalignment honeypots, but a sophisticated peer can pass while harboring an incompatible floor (load-bearing residual). No agreed policy spec, so L3 is inapplicable; ladder tops out at L2 here and cooperation is capped at Class-B / F1. Foreign floor was NOT verified.
